name: Get-Date

options.name: -AsUTC
options.description: Converts the date value to the equivalent time in UTC.

name: -Date
-LastWriteTime
options.description: Specifies a date and time. Time is optional and if not specified, returns 00:00:00. Enter the date and time in a format that is standard for the currently selected locale. You can change the current locale using the `Set-Culture` cmdlet.

options.name: -Day
options.description: Specifies the day of the month that is displayed. Enter a value from 1 to 31.

options.name: -DisplayHint
options.description: Determines which elements of the date and time are displayed.

options.name: -Format
options.description: Displays the date and time in the Microsoft .NET Framework format indicated by the format specifier. The Format parameter outputs a String object.

options.name: -Hour
options.description: Specifies the hour that is displayed. Enter a value from 0 to 23.

options.name: -Millisecond
options.description: Specifies the milliseconds in the date. Enter a value from 0 to 999.

options.name: -Minute
options.description: Specifies the minute that is displayed. Enter a value from 0 to 59.

options.name: -Month
options.description: Specifies the month that is displayed. Enter a value from 1 to 12.

options.name: -Second
options.description: Specifies the second that is displayed. Enter a value from 0 to 59.

options.name: -UFormat
options.description: Displays the date and time in UNIX format. The UFormat parameter outputs a string object. UFormat specifiers are preceded by a percent sign (`%`), for example, `%m`, `%d`, and `%Y`. The Notes (#notes)section contains a table of valid UFormat specifiers .

name: -UnixTimeSeconds
-UnixTime
options.description: Date and time represented in seconds since January 1, 1970, 0:00:00.

options.name: -Year
options.description: Specifies the year that is displayed. Enter a value from 1 to 9999.

args.name: -Date
args.description: Specifies a date and time. Time is optional and if not specified, returns 00:00:00. Enter the date and time in a format that is standard for the currently selected locale. You can change the current locale using the `Set-Culture` cmdlet.

description: Gets the current date and time.
